mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still near but below the inflation rate, markets price a slower path of cuts and a higher terminal rate. That pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next month as front-end expectations reprice upward and term premium follows.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL is stable or falling while FEDFUNDS is unchanged and the 10-year yield falls on 20-day horizons in most triggered periods, reject this mechanism; also reject if the sign of the CPI-FEDFUNDS spread has no positive relation to forward yield changes in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and lowers expected inflation and real growth. That should pull the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days, especially when UNRATE has risen meaningfully from its recent level.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp versus the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are not negative after UNRATE threshold breaches, or if 20-day Treasury yields rise in more than 60% of episodes where UNRATE is at least 0.2pp above its 3-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are accelerating alongside payroll growth, the economy is signaling stronger aggregate demand. That should lift nominal growth expectations and push the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not correspond to higher 20-day Treasury yields, or if each series alone explains the effect with no incremental joint signal in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates stronger real activity and tighter productive utilization. That increases growth and inflation expectations, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly INDPRO gains are not followed by higher 60-day Treasury yields, or if the sign relationship flips negative in the majority of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a forward-looking demand indicator. When HOUST rebounds after weakness, it signals firmer construction demand and higher cyclical growth expectations, which should lift the 10-year yield over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises >= 5% from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebounds are followed by flat or lower 20-day Treasury yields in most cases, or if the effect disappears once the threshold definition is shifted slightly.

mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS remains restrictive while inflation stays above target, investors expect policy to stay tight longer. That should keep 10-year yields elevated or rising over the next 5 trading days through front-end repricing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL is not falling over the latest 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon yield changes do not turn positive when FEDFUNDS is restrictive and inflation is sticky, or if the combined condition adds no explanatory power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor market, falling payroll growth together with a higher unemployment rate signals weakening nominal income growth and lower inflation pressure. That combination should drive the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises versus the prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not predict lower 20-day Treasury yields, or if yield declines occur equally when only one labor series weakens.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is firm and retail sales are rising together, the economy is in an expansionary demand regime that supports higher nominal rates. The 10-year Treasury yield should rise over a 60-trading-day window as the market prices stronger medium-term growth.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-retail sales co-strength does not lead to higher 60-day Treasury yields, or if the relationship weakens to zero in rolling periods after controlling for either proxy alone.
